_comment: The document specifies HBP 1.0 codes M800002, M800009 and HBP 2.0 code MM007A for the same procedure. MM007A is used as the primary procedure code.
condition: Mental and Behavioural disorders due to psychoactive substance use
procedure_name: Mental and Behavioural disorders due to psychoactive substance use
source_files: Mental and Behavioural disorders due to psychoactive substance use_Medco.pdf
Mental and Behavioural disorders due to psychoactive substance use_PPD.CPD.pdf
clinical_indications: Co-morbid significant medical illness and/or psychiatric illness

preauth.label: Clinical notes with detailed history and chronicity
Admission document signed by empanelled psychiatrist

ppd_preauth.q: Clinical notes contain detailed history, mini mental status test, indication for treatment and need of hospitalization?
Was the admission document signed by an empanelled psychiatrist?

cpd_claim.q: Are the detailed treatment notes submitted?
Are the following investigations done: Complete hemogram, Liver function test, Serum electrolytes, Random blood glucose?
Is there a Detailed Discharge Summary mentioning date of follow-up submitted?

common_queries: Patient admission document not signed by an empanelled psychiatrist
Clinical notes missing detailed history, mini mental status test, indication for treatment, or need of hospitalization
Detailed treatment notes not submitted at claim stage
Mandatory investigations (complete hemogram, liver function test, serum electrolytes, random blood glucose) not performed or reports not submitted
Discharge summary missing or does not mention date of follow-up

additional_information.special_empanelment: As per the provisions of the Mental Health Act 2017
clinical_key_pointers: The provisions under Mental Healthcare Act 2017 be referred for details on Admission & Discharge criteria.
additional_information.comorbidity_management: Co-morbid medical illness or psychiatric illness and their treatment; Treatment of additional psychiatric disorder or substance use disorder; Consult with other medical specialists (like gastroenterology or medicine for haematemesis).
referral_criteria: REFER TO SECONDARY CARE IF: H/o withdrawal seizures/hallucinations; Additional psychiatric disorder; Recurrent failed attempts at treatment
REFER TO TERTIARY CARE IF: H/o delirium tremens; Major medical problems; Additional substance use
